# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.
# Category: container_security

#
# Dockerfile to build a Docker-in-Docker client container
#
FROM alpine/git

# Install Docker CLI
RUN apk add --no-cache docker-cli

# Set the working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy any necessary application code (if this is more than just a raw client)
# COPY . .

# Set an entrypoint to keep the container running or to execute a specific command
# For a raw client, a shell might be sufficient, or you can point to your orchestration script
ENTRYPOINT ["sh"]

# Command to be executed by default (e.g., if you want to run a specific script)
# CMD ["./your-orchestration-script.sh"]

# No CMD is fine if the user will override it with `docker run ... bash` or similar
# to manually interact with the daemon.

# Instructions for running this container:
# docker run -it --rm \
#   -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \
#   -v /path/to/your/app:/app \
#   your-image-name
#
# Replace `/path/to/your/app` with the actual path if you're copying scripts.
# The key part is mounting `/var/run/docker.sock`.
